cover:
A high-quality architectural system block diagram illustrating the "RAG FOR SALESFORCE KNOWLEDGE BASE".
Overall Theme: Technical architecture map, clean flow chart, analytical, educational, modern and well-structured layout.
Background: A very clean, subtle light gray background with faint grid lines.

Header:
- At the top-center, a massive dark bold rounded header block displaying "RAG FOR SALESFORCE KNOWLEDGE BASE".
- Below the title, a small subtitle: "Index, Retrieve, and Ground Agentforce 2.0 on Your Own Articles".
- In the top-right corner, a small circular profile icon labeled "Bennie Joseph | Architect".

Architecture Layers (Arranged hierarchically with clear connections):
- A "Salesforce Knowledge Layer" container (colored in neon blue) containing modules: "Published Articles", "Record Types", "Categories", "Versioned Content".
- A "Data Cloud Vector Index" container (colored in neon green) containing modules: "Text Normalization", "Embedding Generation", "VECTOR_DISTANCE Search", "Top-K Retrieval".
- A "Grounded Agent Layer" container (colored in grey) containing modules: "Agentforce 2.0", "claude-sonnet-4-7", "Citation Requirement", "Confidence Gate".
- A central block at the heart of the system labeled "RAG Pipeline" with a rotating arrow icon. This central node has multiple dotted directional connector lines feeding into and out of all surrounding layers.
- An "INTEGRATION LAYER" box on the upper right with modules "Data Cloud Native Vector Search" and "Salesforce API v64.0".
- A bottom panel labeled "RETRIEVAL GUARDRAILS" with sub-modules "Published-Status Filter", "Article Version Check", "customerFacing Flag", "Audit Log".

Style Details: Technical blueprint style, clean rounded rectangle containers, precise dotted lines with arrowheads showing control and data flow, soft pastel color scheme (pale yellows, soft blues, light greens, and dark blues), micro vector icons inside each block, neat modern sans-serif fonts, absolutely crisp rendering, no blurred elements, zero spelling mistakes, publication-ready engineering diagram.
images:
A highly detailed, technical blueprint-style developer infographic poster on the theme of "KNOWLEDGE → VECTOR DB FIELD MAPPING".
Overall Theme: High-tech dark mode developer dashboard, cybersecurity blueprint, with sharp glowing lines and neat borders.
Colors: Deep dark navy background with glowing neon blue, electric cyan, warning red, and success green highlights.

Header:
- A prominent top-center glowing blue header: "KNOWLEDGE → VECTOR DB FIELD MAPPING".
- Below it, a clean subtitle: "Exactly Which Salesforce Knowledge Fields to Index and Why" with a small Salesforce logo on the top-left and an "APEX BEST PRACTICES" badge on the top-right.

Section 1: "WHY IT MATTERS" (Top Row, 3 Horizontal Cards)
- "Clean Text Input" with a filter icon, "512-Token Chunks" with a layers icon, "customerFacing Flag" with a shield icon

Section 2: "THE COST" (Middle Row, Side-by-Side Comparison)
- Left Box (Red Theme): "WRONG: Index Raw Article Body As-Is". Shows a red status pill "DANGER — ANTI-PATTERN".
- Right Box (Green Theme): "RIGHT: Normalize Then Chunk Then Index". Shows a green status pill "SUCCESS — BEST PRACTICE".

Section 3: "CODE PATTERNS" (Main Center)
- Left Column (Bad Code in Red Border):
  - Heading: "// BAD"
  - Code block:
    // Indexing raw HTML Knowledge Article
const text = article.Body__c  // HTML tags, tokens wasted
                               // SOQL, Apex code snippets
                               // boilerplate headers/footers
// Result: poor retrieval, hallucinated citations
- Right Column (Good Code in Green Border):
  - Heading: "// GOOD"
  - Code block:
    const chunks = normalizeAndChunk({
  title: article.Title,
  summary: article.Summary__c,
  body: stripHtml(article.Body__c),
  recordType: article.RecordType.Name,
  version: article.VersionNumber,
  customerFacing: article.IsVisibleInCsp,
})
// Each chunk: 512 tokens, overlapping 50

Section 4: "DEVELOPER PROFILE & CHECKLIST" (Bottom Row)
- Left Side: A 3D Pixar-style cartoon avatar portrait of a dark-skinned South Asian male with a full thick black beard, shoulder-length wavy dark hair pulled up into a messy top bun, warm friendly smile showing teeth, wearing a grey crew-neck sweatshirt with the word "MAGNETIC" on it, set against a warm orange bokeh background. The avatar is labeled "Bennie Joseph | Salesforce Architect". Next to it is a quote bubble: "Architecting intuitive systems, engineering scalable solutions."
- Right Side: A checklist with points: "Strip HTML before embedding — tags waste token budget", "Filter to Published articles only — never index drafts", "Add customerFacing flag — never retrieve internal-only", "Chunk at 512 tokens with 50-token overlap", "Store article version number — detect stale citations".
- Footer: A thin bar with icons for "Data Cloud", "Vector Search", "Knowledge Base", "RAG Pipeline" and the text "CODE SMART. AUTOMATE FASTER. DELIVER EXCELLENCE."

Style Details: Super high resolution, technical drawing grid, razor-sharp white and neon text, crisp layout, developer IDE aesthetic, flat vector graphics with subtle glow, highly legible monospace font for code blocks, professional presentation.

A highly detailed, technical blueprint-style developer infographic poster on the theme of "5 RAG FAILURE MODES IN SALESFORCE".
Overall Theme: High-tech dark mode developer dashboard, cybersecurity blueprint, with sharp glowing lines and neat borders.
Colors: Deep dark navy background with glowing neon blue, electric cyan, warning red, and success green highlights.

Header:
- A prominent top-center glowing blue header: "5 RAG FAILURE MODES IN SALESFORCE".
- Below it, a clean subtitle: "What Goes Wrong in Production and How to Prevent Each One" with a small Salesforce logo on the top-left and an "APEX BEST PRACTICES" badge on the top-right.

Section 1: "WHY IT MATTERS" (Top Row, 3 Horizontal Cards)
- "Published Filter" with a checkmark icon, "Version Freshness" with a clock icon, "Score Threshold" with a filter icon

Section 2: "THE COST" (Middle Row, Side-by-Side Comparison)
- Left Box (Red Theme): "FAILURE MODES — Real Production Issues". Shows a red status pill "DANGER — ANTI-PATTERN".
- Right Box (Green Theme): "PREVENTION — What Actually Works". Shows a green status pill "SUCCESS — BEST PRACTICE".

Section 3: "CODE PATTERNS" (Main Center)
- Left Column (Bad Code in Red Border):
  - Heading: "// BAD"
  - Code block:
    // All 5 failure modes in one bad design:
1. No published-status filter → draft articles retrieved
2. No customerFacing check → internal notes in emails
3. No version check → outdated policy cited confidently
4. No chunk overlap → context split across boundaries
5. No confidence gate → 0.3 score article still used
- Right Column (Good Code in Green Border):
  - Heading: "// GOOD"
  - Code block:
    WHERE IsPublished = true
  AND IsVisibleInCsp = true          // customer-safe
  AND VersionNumber = latestVersion  // not stale
ORDER BY VECTOR_DISTANCE(...) LIMIT 10
// Then: if (topScore < 0.75) escalate
// Never cite if customerFacing = false

Section 4: "DEVELOPER PROFILE & CHECKLIST" (Bottom Row)
- Left Side: A 3D Pixar-style cartoon avatar portrait of a dark-skinned South Asian male with a full thick black beard, shoulder-length wavy dark hair pulled up into a messy top bun, warm friendly smile showing teeth, wearing a grey crew-neck sweatshirt with the word "MAGNETIC" on it, set against a warm orange bokeh background. The avatar is labeled "Bennie Joseph | Salesforce Architect". Next to it is a quote bubble: "Architecting intuitive systems, engineering scalable solutions."
- Right Side: A checklist with points: "Always filter IsPublished = true and IsVisibleInCsp = true", "Check VersionNumber matches current published version", "Reject retrieval results below 0.75 similarity score", "Log which article versions were cited per agent run", "Re-embed when articles are updated — stale index = wrong answers".
- Footer: A thin bar with icons for "RAG Quality", "Data Freshness", "Citation Safety", "Vector Search" and the text "CODE SMART. AUTOMATE FASTER. DELIVER EXCELLENCE."

Style Details: Super high resolution, technical drawing grid, razor-sharp white and neon text, crisp layout, developer IDE aesthetic, flat vector graphics with subtle glow, highly legible monospace font for code blocks, professional presentation.
